name: mocking
context: Mocking strategies — stubs, spies, fakes, dependency injection, and when to mock vs use real implementations
info: Mocking isolates the unit under test by replacing dependencies with controlled substitutes. This node covers the spectrum of test doubles (stubs, spies, mocks, fakes), framework-specific mocking APIs ([[testing/jest]] jest.mock, [[testing/vitest]] vi.mock, [[testing/pytest]] pytest-mock/monkeypatch), dependency injection for testability, and the critical decision of when to mock versus when to use real implementations. Over-mocking leads to tests that pass but don't catch bugs. Under-mocking leads to slow, flaky tests. Agents use this node to learn the right mocking strategy for each situation.
triggers: mock an api call in tests

sections.title: Types of test doubles
sections.body: Four types of test doubles: **Stubs** return canned data (mockResolvedValue, return_value). **Spies** record calls while delegating to real code (jest.spyOn, vi.spyOn). **Mocks** combine stubs and spies — controlled output plus call verification. **Fakes** are simplified working implementations (in-memory database, fake SMTP server). Choose based on what you need: if you need controlled output, stub. If you need to verify interaction, spy. If you need both, mock. If you need realistic behavior without the real dependency, fake.

sections.title: Jest mocking API
sections.body: [[testing/jest]] provides: `jest.fn()` creates a mock function. `jest.mock('./module')` replaces an entire module. `jest.spyOn(obj, 'method')` spies on a method. `mockReturnValue()`, `mockResolvedValue()` control return values. `mockImplementation()` replaces the full implementation. Manual mocks in `__mocks__/` provide module-level defaults. Always call `mockRestore()` or use `restoreAllMocks` in config to prevent mock leakage between tests.

sections.title: Vitest mocking API
sections.body: [[testing/vitest]] provides: `vi.fn()` creates a mock function. `vi.mock('./module', () => ({ ... }))` replaces a module with a factory. `vi.spyOn(obj, 'method')` spies. `vi.mocked(fn)` provides type-safe access to mock methods. `vi.useFakeTimers()` / `vi.advanceTimersByTime()` for timer mocking. `vi.stubGlobal('fetch', fn)` for global stubs. The API is intentionally Jest-compatible — migration is mostly replacing `jest.` with `vi.`.

sections.title: Python mocking with pytest
sections.body: [[testing/pytest]] offers three mocking approaches: **pytest-mock** provides the `mocker` fixture wrapping unittest.mock — use `mocker.patch('module.path.function')`. **monkeypatch** (built-in) sets attributes, env vars, and dict items — use for simple replacements. **unittest.mock** directly with `@patch` decorator or `patch()` context manager. Key rule: patch where a name is *used*, not where it's *defined*. For async code, use `AsyncMock`. `mocker.patch.object(instance, 'method')` for instance methods.

sections.title: HTTP and API mocking
sections.body: For JavaScript/TypeScript: **msw** (Mock Service Worker) intercepts HTTP at the network level — works with fetch, axios, and any HTTP client. Define handlers with `http.get()`, `http.post()`, etc. Use `setupServer()` for Node tests, `setupWorker()` for browser tests. For Python: **responses** mocks the `requests` library. **httpx-mock** mocks `httpx`. **aioresponses** mocks `aiohttp`. For [[testing/playwright]]: `page.route()` intercepts browser network requests directly.

sections.title: When to mock vs. use real implementations
sections.body: Mock at system boundaries: external APIs, databases (in unit tests), file systems, time, randomness, third-party services. Don't mock: the subject under test, simple value objects, standard library functions, your own code in integration tests. The heuristic: if mocking a dependency makes the test faster and more deterministic without hiding real bugs, mock it. If mocking hides integration issues or makes the test verify implementation details instead of behavior, don't mock. Over-mocked tests pass when code is broken. Under-mocked tests are slow and flaky.
